articles.headline: PM Launches 'National Urban Renewal Mission 2.0' for Sustainable City Development
articles.explainer:
• Prime Minister unveils an ambitious second phase of the urban development mission, focusing on climate-resilient infrastructure and smart governance.
• The mission aims to transform 100 cities into 'Green & Smart Hubs' over the next five years, integrating renewable energy and digital solutions.
• Key pillars include integrated waste management, circular economy principles, green mobility solutions, and enhanced public-private partnerships.
• Funding mechanisms include a mix of central assistance, state contributions, and innovative municipal bonds tied to green projects.
• Emphasis on citizen participation and capacity building for urban local bodies to ensure long-term sustainability.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
Urbanization, their problems and their remedies
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: National Urban Renewal Mission 2.0 launched for 100 cities.
Focus on climate-resilient infrastructure, green mobility, waste management.
Funding through central, state, and green municipal bonds.
Aims for 'Green & Smart Hubs' transformation over five years.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolution of India's urban development policies and the need for Mission 2.0.
Analyze the potential impact of green infrastructure and digital solutions on urban livability and sustainability.
Critically evaluate the role of public-private partnerships and citizen participation in achieving mission objectives.
Examine the challenges in implementing large-scale urban transformation projects in diverse Indian cities.
articles.imageDescription: Prime Minister addressing a conference on sustainable urban development, with a digital rendering of a smart city in the background.
articles.trivia: India is projected to have 600 million people living in urban areas by 2030, making sustainable urban planning critical for its future.

articles.headline: India Hosts G20 Sherpa Meeting Ahead of Annual Summit, Focus on Global South Agenda
articles.explainer:
• India chairs the second G20 Sherpa meeting in Udaipur, setting the agenda for the upcoming annual leaders' summit.
• Discussions primarily focused on strengthening multilateral institutions, climate finance for developing nations, and food security.
• India reiterated its commitment to amplifying the voice of the Global South within the G20 framework.
• Key outcomes include consensus building on sustainable development goals and a roadmap for digital public infrastructure sharing.
• The meeting also addressed global economic stability amidst ongoing geopolitical challenges and supply chain disruptions.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
prelimsPoints: Second G20 Sherpa meeting held in Udaipur, chaired by India.
Agenda includes climate finance, food security, multilateral institution reform.
India championing the voice of the Global South.
Consensus on SDGs and Digital Public Infrastructure sharing roadmap.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's increasing role in global governance through platforms like G20.
Discuss the significance of India's 'Global South' agenda in shaping international discourse and policy.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities for G20 in addressing contemporary global economic and environmental issues.
Examine how digital public infrastructure can be leveraged for global sustainable development.
articles.imageDescription: Delegates from G20 nations participating in a high-level meeting in a conference hall.
articles.trivia: The G20 represents around two-thirds of the world's population, 85% of global GDP, and over 75% of global trade.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate, Signals Cautious Optimism Amidst Global Headwinds
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5%.
• This decision reflects a cautious approach, balancing inflation management with supporting economic growth.
• RBI Governor highlighted improving domestic consumption and investment sentiment, while acknowledging persistent global economic uncertainties.
• Inflation projections for FY25 were revised marginally downwards, attributing to easing supply-side pressures.
• The central bank reiterated its commitment to remaining 'withdrawal of accommodation' to ensure inflation aligns with the target.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Monetary Policy.
prelimsPoints: RBI MPC maintains repo rate at 6.5%.
Inflation projections for FY25 revised downwards.
Policy stance remains 'withdrawal of accommodation'.
Focus on balancing inflation control and growth support.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors influencing RBI's decision to maintain the repo rate and its implications for the Indian economy.
Discuss the challenges faced by the MPC in balancing inflation control with economic growth objectives.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current monetary policy tools in addressing domestic and global economic uncertainties.
Examine the relationship between government fiscal policy and RBI's monetary policy in achieving macroeconomic stability.
articles.imageDescription: The Reserve Bank of India headquarters building in Mumbai.
articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was constituted in 2016 to bring transparency and accountability in formulating monetary policy.

articles.headline: India Unveils Aggressive Targets for Green Hydrogen Production by 2030
articles.explainer:
• Ministry of New and Renewable Energy announces revised ambitious targets under the National Green Hydrogen Mission.
• India aims to achieve 15 million tonnes of green hydrogen production capacity by 2030, significantly increasing from previous targets.
• The move is intended to position India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, reducing reliance on fossil fuels.
• New incentives for electrolyser manufacturing and green hydrogen adoption in hard-to-abate sectors like steel and fertilizers were proposed.
• This initiative is crucial for India's net-zero emissions goal by 2070 and enhancing energy security.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
articles.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
prelimsPoints: New target: 15 million tonnes green hydrogen production by 2030.
Part of National Green Hydrogen Mission.
Aims to make India a global hub for production and export.
Incentives for electrolyser manufacturing and sectoral adoption.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of green hydrogen for India's energy security and climate goals.
Analyze the economic and technological challenges in scaling up green hydrogen production and adoption in India.
Evaluate the potential of India becoming a global leader in green hydrogen, considering domestic demand and export potential.
Examine the role of government incentives and policy frameworks in driving the green hydrogen ecosystem.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized graphic showing hydrogen atoms and green energy sources like wind turbines and solar panels.
articles.trivia: Green hydrogen is produced by splitting water into hydrogen and oxygen using renewable electricity, making it a zero-emission fuel.

articles.headline: ISRO's 'Shukrayaan-1' Completes Critical Orbital Maneuver Towards Venus
articles.explainer:
• India's maiden mission to Venus, 'Shukrayaan-1', successfully executed a crucial Trans-Venus Injection (TVI) maneuver.
• This maneuver propels the spacecraft out of Earth's sphere of influence and towards its intended trajectory to Venus.
• ISRO scientists expressed confidence in the mission's progress, which aims to study Venus's atmosphere and surface composition.
• 'Shukrayaan-1' carries 16 scientific payloads from India, France, Sweden, and Germany, making it a truly international collaboration.
• The mission is expected to reach Venus orbit by late 2025, providing valuable data on the planet's geological and atmospheric evolution.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space.
prelimsPoints: ISRO's 'Shukrayaan-1' is India's first Venus mission.
Successfully completed Trans-Venus Injection (TVI) maneuver.
Carries 16 scientific payloads from multiple countries.
Aims to study Venus's atmosphere and surface.
Expected to reach Venus orbit by late 2025.
mainsPoints: Discuss the scientific objectives of ISRO's Shukrayaan-1 mission and its significance for planetary science.
Analyze how international collaborations enhance India's space exploration capabilities and global standing.
Evaluate the technological challenges involved in interplanetary missions and ISRO's advancements in this domain.
Examine the broader implications of such missions for understanding Earth's climate and geological history.
articles.imageDescription: An artistic rendering of the Shukrayaan-1 spacecraft orbiting Venus, with its scientific instruments deployed.
articles.trivia: Venus is often called Earth's 'sister planet' due to its similar size and mass, but it has an extremely hot and dense atmosphere.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Validity of 'One Nation, One Election' Committee Recommendations
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a landmark ruling, has affirmed the constitutional validity of the 'One Nation, One Election' committee's recommendations.
• The five-judge constitutional bench stated that simultaneous elections align with the spirit of cooperative federalism, provided certain constitutional amendments are enacted.
• The judgment emphasizes the need for broad political consensus and extensive public deliberation before full implementation.
• Arguments for the move include significant cost savings, reduced burden on administrative machinery, and continuity of policy.
• Critics' concerns regarding potential impact on federal structure and regional parties were acknowledged, with the court suggesting safeguards.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act.
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court upheld constitutional validity of 'One Nation, One Election' recommendations.
Requires constitutional amendments and political consensus.
Arguments: cost savings, administrative efficiency, policy continuity.
Court acknowledged concerns regarding federal structure and regional parties.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the pros and cons of implementing 'One Nation, One Election' in India.
Discuss the constitutional challenges and amendments required for simultaneous elections.
Evaluate the potential impact of simultaneous elections on India's federal structure and the dynamics of regional politics.
Examine the role of the Election Commission of India and other stakeholders in facilitating such a reform.
articles.imageDescription: A gavel resting on law books in front of the Supreme Court of India emblem.
articles.trivia: India held simultaneous elections for Lok Sabha and State Assemblies until 1967.

articles.headline: Indian Navy Inducts First Indigenous Stealth Destroyer from Project 15B
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Navy commissioned 'INS Visakhapatnam-II', the first of four stealth destroyers built under Project 15B, at Mazagon Dock Shipbuilders Limited.
• This induction marks a significant milestone in India's 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' (Self-Reliant India) initiative in defense manufacturing.
• The destroyer is equipped with advanced stealth features, state-of-the-art weapon systems, and sensors, enhancing India's maritime capabilities.
• Project 15B follows the successful Project 15A (Kolkata-class) destroyers, building on indigenous design and construction expertise.
• The vessel will significantly bolster the Indian Navy's presence in the Indo-Pacific region, contributing to regional security.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
prelimsPoints: INS Visakhapatnam-II, first Project 15B stealth destroyer, commissioned.
Built by Mazagon Dock Shipbuilders Limited (MDL).
Features advanced stealth technology and weapon systems.
Part of 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' in defense manufacturing.
Enhances Indian Navy's capabilities in the Indo-Pacific.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic significance of indigenous defense production for India's national security and geopolitical ambitions.
Analyze the role of Project 15B destroyers in enhancing the Indian Navy's blue-water capabilities and regional deterrence.
Evaluate the progress and challenges in achieving self-reliance in India's defense sector.
Examine the implications of a stronger Indian Navy for stability in the Indo-Pacific region.
articles.imageDescription: The newly commissioned INS Visakhapatnam-II destroyer sailing in open waters.
articles.trivia: Stealth technology in naval vessels aims to reduce their radar, infrared, acoustic, and magnetic signatures, making them harder to detect.

articles.headline: New National Education Policy Implementation Enters Advanced Phase with Focus on Skill Development
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education announces the advanced phase of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 implementation, prioritizing skill development.
• New guidelines issued for integrating vocational education and skill training from the school level (Class 6 onwards) into higher education.
• Emphasis on practical learning, internships, and industry partnerships to make graduates job-ready and foster entrepreneurship.
• The National Credit Framework (NCrF) is being fully operationalized to facilitate seamless mobility between academic and vocational streams.
• Teacher training programs are being revamped to equip educators with skills for multidisciplinary and experiential learning approaches.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 implementation enters advanced phase, focusing on skill development.
Vocational education from Class 6, integration into higher education.
National Credit Framework (NCrF) fully operationalized.
Emphasis on internships and industry partnerships.
mainsPoints: Discuss the core objectives of NEP 2020 regarding skill development and its potential to address India's demographic dividend.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in integrating vocational education into the mainstream curriculum.
Evaluate the significance of the National Credit Framework (NCrF) in promoting flexibility and multidisciplinary learning.
Examine the role of teacher training and industry collaboration in successful implementation of skill-based education.
articles.imageDescription: Students engaged in hands-on vocational training in a modern classroom setting.
articles.trivia: The NEP 2020 replaced the 1986 National Policy on Education, aiming for a holistic, flexible, and multidisciplinary education system.

articles.headline: India-EU Free Trade Agreement Talks Progress Towards Critical Breakthrough
articles.explainer:
• Negotiations for the India-European Union Free Trade Agreement (FTA) are reportedly nearing a breakthrough on key contentious issues.
• Both sides have made significant progress on market access for goods, services, and investment protection.
• Areas of consensus include intellectual property rights, geographical indications, and sustainable development provisions.
• The FTA is expected to significantly boost bilateral trade and investment, creating new opportunities for businesses.
• Despite initial hurdles, political will from both India and the EU is driving the accelerated pace of negotiations.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India-EU FTA talks nearing breakthrough on key issues.
Progress on market access for goods, services, investment.
Consensus on IPR, GIs, sustainable development provisions.
Aims to boost bilateral trade and investment.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic and economic significance of an India-EU FTA for India's global trade ambitions.
Discuss the potential benefits and challenges for Indian industries and consumers from the FTA.
Evaluate the role of intellectual property rights and sustainable development clauses in modern trade agreements.
Examine how India balances its 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' vision with the pursuit of global trade integration.
articles.imageDescription: The flags of India and the European Union displayed together, symbolizing cooperation.
articles.trivia: The EU is one of India's largest trading partners, and an FTA would significantly liberalize trade in goods and services.

articles.headline: Project Cheetah Expansion: New Batch Arrives at Kuno National Park
articles.explainer:
• A fresh batch of 12 cheetahs, sourced from South Africa and Namibia, arrived at Kuno National Park, Madhya Pradesh.
• This marks the third phase of Project Cheetah, aiming to establish a viable and self-sustaining cheetah population in India.
• The new arrivals bring the total cheetah count at Kuno to over 30, following earlier successful translocations.
• Intensive monitoring and veterinary care protocols are in place to ensure the acclimatization and survival of the new cheetahs.
• The project is a flagship initiative for biodiversity conservation and restoring ecological balance in Indian grasslands.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Biodiversity and its conservation.
articles.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: New batch of 12 cheetahs arrived at Kuno National Park.
Part of the third phase of Project Cheetah.
Total cheetah count at Kuno now over 30.
Cheetahs sourced from South Africa and Namibia.
Aims for a self-sustaining cheetah population in India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance and conservation objectives of Project Cheetah in India.
Analyze the challenges involved in reintroducing a species like the cheetah, including habitat management and prey base.
Evaluate the role of international collaboration in wildlife conservation efforts, citing Project Cheetah as an example.
Examine the socio-economic impacts and community involvement aspects of large-scale wildlife projects.
articles.imageDescription: A newly arrived cheetah cautiously stepping out of its crate into an enclosure at Kuno National Park.
articles.trivia: Cheetahs were declared extinct in India in 1952, making Project Cheetah a historic effort to bring them back.

articles.headline: Digital Public Infrastructure: UPI Transactions Cross New Milestones, Boosting Financial Inclusion
articles.explainer:
• Unified Payments Interface (UPI) recorded over 15 billion transactions in January 2025, reaching a new monthly high.
• This surge highlights the growing adoption of digital payments across urban and rural India, driving financial inclusion.
• The success of UPI is attributed to its user-friendliness, interoperability, and low-cost transaction model.
• Government initiatives like 'Digital India' and 'Jan Dhan Yojana' have created a conducive environment for DPI adoption.
• The expansion of UPI-linked services, including credit on UPI and cross-border payments, continues to enhance its utility.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: UPI transactions crossed 15 billion in January 2025.
Highlights growing adoption of digital payments in India.
Success attributed to user-friendliness, interoperability, low cost.
Supported by 'Digital India' and 'Jan Dhan Yojana' initiatives.
Expansion includes credit on UPI and cross-border payments.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative impact of Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) like UPI on India's financial landscape and economy.
Analyze how UPI contributes to financial inclusion, especially in rural and underserved areas.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in further expanding India's digital payments ecosystem.
Examine the lessons other developing countries can learn from India's UPI success story.
articles.imageDescription: A person using a smartphone to make a UPI payment at a local vendor, illustrating digital transactions.
articles.trivia: UPI was launched in 2016 and has rapidly become the dominant digital payment system in India.

articles.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends Reforms for Lokpal and Lokayukta Act
articles.explainer:
• A Joint Parliamentary Committee submitted its report recommending significant amendments to the Lokpal and Lokayukta Act, 2013.
• Key recommendations include strengthening the investigative powers of the Lokpal and ensuring its financial and administrative autonomy.
• The panel suggested a more streamlined process for the appointment of Lokpal members and clearer guidelines for their jurisdiction.
• Focus on enhancing transparency and accountability mechanisms for public functionaries, including provisions for quicker disposal of complaints.
• The recommendations aim to make the anti-corruption ombudsman more effective and independent in combating corruption.
gsSubTopics: Statutory, regulatory and various quasi-judicial bodies.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Transparency & Accountability and institutional and other measures.
prelimsPoints: Parliamentary Committee recommended amendments to Lokpal and Lokayukta Act, 2013.
Suggestions to strengthen Lokpal's investigative powers and autonomy.
Streamlined appointment process and clearer jurisdiction.
Aim to enhance transparency and quicker complaint disposal.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolution and challenges in the functioning of the Lokpal and Lokayukta institutions in India.
Analyze the significance of the parliamentary committee's recommendations in strengthening anti-corruption mechanisms.
Evaluate the balance between ensuring autonomy of anti-corruption bodies and maintaining accountability.
Examine how an effective Lokpal can contribute to good governance and public trust.
articles.imageDescription: The Parliament House of India, symbolizing legislative action and governance.
articles.trivia: The concept of an ombudsman originated in Sweden in the early 19th century to oversee governmental actions.

articles.headline: AI in Healthcare: Government Launches Pilot Projects for Diagnostic Support in Rural Areas
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare, in collaboration with NITI Aayog, initiated pilot projects deploying AI for diagnostic support.
• These projects focus on leveraging AI tools for early detection of diseases like tuberculosis, retinopathy, and certain cancers in remote rural clinics.
• AI-powered platforms assist frontline health workers in interpreting medical images and patient data, compensating for specialist shortages.
• The initiative aims to bridge the healthcare accessibility gap and improve diagnostic accuracy in underserved regions.
• Data privacy and ethical considerations for AI deployment in sensitive healthcare domains are being carefully addressed.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
articles.source: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
prelimsPoints: Pilot projects launched for AI in diagnostic support in rural areas.
Collaboration between Ministry of Health and NITI Aayog.
Focus on early detection of diseases like TB, retinopathy, cancers.
Aims to bridge healthcare accessibility gap.
Ethical considerations for AI deployment being addressed.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of Artificial Intelligence (AI) to transform healthcare delivery in India, particularly in rural settings.
Analyze the benefits and challenges of deploying AI for diagnostic support, considering issues of data accuracy and infrastructure.
Evaluate the ethical implications and data privacy concerns associated with AI in healthcare and proposed safeguards.
Examine how such technological interventions align with India's broader health policy goals for universal healthcare access.
articles.imageDescription: A doctor and a rural health worker looking at an AI-powered diagnostic interface on a tablet, assisting in patient care.
articles.trivia: AI algorithms can analyze medical images like X-rays and CT scans with accuracy comparable to, or sometimes exceeding, human specialists.

articles.headline: India Deepens Strategic Partnership with African Nations at Bilateral Summit
articles.explainer:
• India hosted the 5th India-Africa Forum Summit (IAFS) in New Delhi, reaffirming its commitment to a long-term strategic partnership.
• Key agreements were signed across sectors including defense, renewable energy, digital cooperation, and capacity building.
• Prime Minister emphasized India's 'SAGAR' vision and 'Look Africa' policy, promoting shared prosperity and security in the Indian Ocean region.
• Discussions focused on multilateral cooperation, climate change, counter-terrorism, and strengthening South-South cooperation.
• The summit concluded with a joint declaration outlining a roadmap for enhanced engagement and mutual development goals.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: 5th India-Africa Forum Summit (IAFS) held in New Delhi.
Agreements signed in defense, renewable energy, digital cooperation.
India's 'SAGAR' vision and 'Look Africa' policy highlighted.
Focus on climate change, counter-terrorism, South-South cooperation.
Joint declaration for enhanced engagement and development goals.
mainsPoints: Analyze the historical evolution and current significance of India's relations with African nations.
Discuss the pillars of India's 'Look Africa' policy and its alignment with India's geopolitical and economic interests.
Evaluate the impact of India's development cooperation model in Africa, contrasting it with other global powers.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for strengthening India-Africa collaboration in a rapidly changing global order.
articles.imageDescription: Leaders from India and various African nations shaking hands at the India-Africa Forum Summit.
articles.trivia: India has extended over $12 billion in concessional credit to African countries, supporting various development projects.

articles.headline: ASI Unearths Ancient Buddhist Monastery Remains in Odisha, Boosting Heritage Tourism
articles.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) announced the discovery of extensive remains of an ancient Buddhist monastery in Odisha's Jajpur district.
• The excavations revealed stupas, prayer halls, residential cells, and numerous artifacts dating back to the 7th-10th centuries CE.
• This discovery sheds new light on the spread of Buddhism in eastern India and the region's rich cultural heritage.
• The site is believed to be part of the ancient 'Pushpagiri Mahavihara' complex, a significant learning center mentioned in historical texts.
• The state government plans to develop the site as a major heritage tourism destination, attracting scholars and tourists alike.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.

Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including water-bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes.
prelimsPoints: ASI unearthed ancient Buddhist monastery in Jajpur, Odisha.
Remains include stupas, prayer halls, artifacts from 7th-10th centuries CE.
Believed to be part of Pushpagiri Mahavihara.
Discovery sheds light on Buddhism in eastern India.
Site to be developed for heritage tourism.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of recent archaeological discoveries in understanding India's ancient history and cultural linkages.
Analyze the role of Buddhist monasteries as centers of learning and their contribution to Indian art and architecture.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in preserving and promoting archaeological sites for heritage tourism.
Examine the potential socio-economic impact of developing such sites on local communities.
articles.imageDescription: Archaeologists carefully excavating ancient ruins, revealing stone structures and artifacts at the site.
articles.trivia: Odisha, historically known as Kalinga, was a significant center for the spread of Buddhism in ancient times, with many monastic complexes.

articles.headline: Editorial: The Imperative of Water Conservation in India's Growth Story
articles.explainer:
• India faces increasing water stress due to population growth, urbanization, and climate change, threatening its developmental trajectory.
• The editorial highlights the need for a comprehensive national water policy integrating supply management, demand-side efficiency, and equitable distribution.
• Emphasizes the role of traditional water harvesting techniques, modern irrigation methods, and wastewater recycling in sustainable water management.
• Calls for greater community participation and inter-state cooperation to resolve water disputes and implement conservation strategies effectively.
• Argues that water security is fundamental to food security, public health, and industrial growth, making conservation an economic imperative.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Disaster and disaster management.
Issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
prelimsPoints: India faces severe water stress due to various factors.
Need for comprehensive national water policy.
Emphasizes traditional harvesting, modern irrigation, wastewater recycling.
Calls for community participation and inter-state cooperation.
Water security linked to food security, health, industrial growth.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-faceted challenges leading to water stress in India and their implications for sustainable development.
Critically analyze the effectiveness of existing water management policies and suggest reforms.
Examine the role of traditional knowledge and modern technology in achieving water security in diverse geographical regions of India.
Evaluate the importance of inter-state cooperation and community involvement in resolving water disputes and implementing conservation measures.
articles.imageDescription: A parched agricultural field contrasting with a lush, irrigated farm, symbolizing the impact of water management.
articles.trivia: India, with 18% of the world's population, has only 4% of the world's freshwater resources.

articles.headline: Government Launches 'Sustainability Index' for PSUs to Promote ESG Compliance
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Finance introduced a 'Sustainability Index' for Public Sector Undertakings (PSUs) across various sectors.
• The index will evaluate PSUs based on their Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) performance metrics.
• This initiative aims to encourage PSUs to adopt sustainable business practices and enhance their long-term value creation.
• Performance on the index will be linked to future investment decisions, funding, and performance appraisals for PSU management.
• It reflects India's growing commitment to responsible business conduct and sustainable finance principles.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Environmental pollution and degradation.
prelimsPoints: Ministry of Finance launched 'Sustainability Index' for PSUs.
Evaluates PSUs on Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) metrics.
Aims to promote sustainable business practices.
Performance linked to investment decisions and appraisals.
Reflects commitment to responsible business and sustainable finance.
mainsPoints: Discuss the rationale behind introducing a Sustainability Index for PSUs and its expected impact on their performance.
Analyze the significance of ESG compliance for businesses in the contemporary global economic landscape.
Evaluate the challenges PSUs might face in integrating ESG principles into their operations and reporting.
Examine how this initiative aligns with India's broader goals of sustainable development and responsible capitalism.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic showing interconnected gears with 'Environment', 'Social', and 'Governance' labels, representing ESG factors.
articles.trivia: ESG investing has grown significantly globally, with investors increasingly considering these factors in addition to traditional financial metrics.

articles.headline: India and Sri Lanka Enhance Maritime Security Cooperation in Indian Ocean Region
articles.explainer:
• India and Sri Lanka concluded a high-level dialogue on maritime security, reaffirming their commitment to a free and open Indo-Pacific.
• Discussions focused on intelligence sharing, joint patrols, and capacity building for combating illegal fishing, drug trafficking, and maritime terrorism.
• Both nations emphasized the importance of adhering to international law, including UNCLOS, for regional peace and stability.
• India extended further assistance to the Sri Lankan Navy for training and equipment upgrades.
• The enhanced cooperation is crucial for securing vital sea lanes of communication and addressing common security challenges in the Indian Ocean.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
prelimsPoints: India-Sri Lanka held high-level dialogue on maritime security.
Focus on intelligence sharing, joint patrols, capacity building.
Combatting illegal fishing, drug trafficking, maritime terrorism.
India provided assistance to Sri Lankan Navy.
Crucial for securing sea lanes in Indian Ocean.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving maritime security challenges in the Indian Ocean Region and the role of India-Sri Lanka cooperation.
Analyze the significance of bilateral defense and security partnerships for India's 'Neighbourhood First' policy.
Evaluate the challenges posed by illegal fishing and drug trafficking in the IOR and the effectiveness of current counter-measures.
Examine the importance of UNCLOS in governing maritime activities and ensuring regional stability.
articles.imageDescription: Naval ships from India and Sri Lanka conducting a joint exercise in the Indian Ocean.
articles.trivia: The Indian Ocean is one of the busiest shipping lanes in the world, carrying a significant portion of global trade.

articles.headline: New Species of Frog Discovered in Western Ghats, Highlights Biodiversity Hotspot Status
articles.explainer:
• Scientists from the Zoological Survey of India announced the discovery of a new species of tree frog in the biodiversity-rich Western Ghats.
• The discovery underscores the immense, yet underexplored, biodiversity of the Western Ghats, a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
• The new species, named 'Raorchestes karthikae', is critically endangered due to habitat loss and climate change impacts.
• Researchers emphasized the urgent need for enhanced conservation efforts and protection of fragile ecosystems in the region.
• This finding contributes to a deeper understanding of amphibian evolution and distribution patterns in tropical rainforests.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Biodiversity and its conservation.
prelimsPoints: New tree frog species 'Raorchestes karthikae' discovered in Western Ghats.
Discovery highlights Western Ghats as a biodiversity hotspot.
Species is critically endangered due to habitat loss.
Emphasizes need for conservation efforts.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of the Western Ghats as a biodiversity hotspot and the threats it faces.
Analyze the importance of discovering new species for scientific research and conservation planning.
Evaluate the impact of habitat loss and climate change on amphibian populations in tropical regions.
Examine the policy measures and community involvement necessary for effective biodiversity conservation in sensitive ecosystems.
articles.imageDescription: A close-up photograph of the newly discovered 'Raorchestes karthikae' tree frog on a leaf.
articles.trivia: The Western Ghats is home to at least 325 globally threatened species, making it one of the world's top biodiversity hotspots.

highlights: PM launches 'National Urban Renewal Mission 2.0' for sustainable city development, targeting 100 'Green & Smart Hubs'.
India hosts G20 Sherpa meeting, emphasizing Global South agenda and progress on climate finance and digital public infrastructure.
RBI maintains repo rate at 6.5%, signaling cautious optimism amid global economic uncertainties.
ISRO's 'Shukrayaan-1' successfully completes critical Trans-Venus Injection maneuver, advancing India's maiden Venus mission.
Supreme Court upholds constitutional validity of 'One Nation, One Election' committee recommendations, paving way for electoral reforms.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the 'National Urban Renewal Mission 2.0' recently launched, consider the following statements:
1. The mission aims to transform 50 cities into 'Green & Smart Hubs' over the next three years.
2. It emphasizes integrated waste management, green mobility solutions, and public-private partnerships.
3. Funding mechanisms for the mission will primarily rely on external aid from international agencies.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect: The mission aims to transform 100 cities over five years, not 50 over three. Statement 2 is correct: The explainer clearly mentions these as key pillars. Statement 3 is incorrect: Funding mechanisms include central assistance, state contributions, and innovative municipal bonds, not primarily external aid.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following is the primary objective of ISRO's 'Shukrayaan-1' mission?
options:
(a) To study the surface composition of Mars and search for signs of past water.
(b) To explore Jupiter's icy moons for subsurface oceans.
(c) To study Venus's atmosphere and surface composition.
(d) To deploy a new generation of communication satellites in Earth's orbit.
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) To study Venus's atmosphere and surface composition.
prelimsMocks.explanation: ISRO's 'Shukrayaan-1' is India's maiden mission to Venus, specifically designed to study Venus's atmosphere and surface composition. Options (a) and (b) refer to missions to Mars and Jupiter, respectively. Option (d) describes a different type of space mission.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the 'One Nation, One Election' concept:
1. It proposes simultaneous elections for the Lok Sabha and State Legislative Assemblies.
2. The Supreme Court has recently declared it unconstitutional due to concerns about federalism.
3. Arguments in its favor include significant cost savings and reduced burden on administrative machinery.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: 'One Nation, One Election' proposes simultaneous elections. Statement 2 is incorrect: The Supreme Court upheld its constitutional validity, albeit with conditions for amendments and consensus. Statement 3 is correct: Cost savings and administrative efficiency are key arguments in its favor.

prelimsMocks.question: Project Cheetah, which recently saw the arrival of a new batch of cheetahs at Kuno National Park, aims to reintroduce cheetahs sourced from which countries?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The article states that the new batch of cheetahs for Project Cheetah was sourced from South Africa and Namibia, continuing the trend from previous phases of the project.

prelimsMocks.question: The newly discovered tree frog species 'Raorchestes karthikae' was found in which of the following biodiversity hotspots of India?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The article explicitly mentions that the new species of tree frog was discovered in the biodiversity-rich Western Ghats, a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

mainsMocks.question: India's urban centers are critical engines of economic growth but face significant sustainability challenges. In light of the newly launched 'National Urban Renewal Mission 2.0', critically analyze its potential to foster climate-resilient and inclusive urban development. (15 marks, 250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors; Urbanization, their problems and their remedies. GS3: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the mission and its objectives. Discuss the key pillars like green infrastructure, smart governance, and citizen participation. Analyze its potential benefits for climate resilience, economic growth, and social inclusion. Also, address potential challenges in implementation, funding, and coordination among various stakeholders. Conclude with the importance of a holistic approach.

mainsMocks.question: The success of India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) like UPI has been lauded globally. Discuss how DPI contributes to financial inclusion and economic growth in India. What lessons can other developing countries draw from India's experience in building robust DPI? (15 marks, 250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment; Inclusive growth and issues arising from it; Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
mainsMocks.approach: Define DPI and highlight UPI's achievements. Explain how UPI promotes financial inclusion (e.g., ease of access, low cost, reach) and contributes to economic growth (e.g., formalization, small business support, innovation). Discuss the factors behind UPI's success (e.g., government support, interoperability, robust tech). Conclude by outlining actionable lessons for other developing nations, such as focusing on public good, open architecture, and regulatory support.

mainsMocks.question: India's push for green hydrogen production is a strategic move towards energy security and net-zero emissions. Evaluate the opportunities and challenges for India in becoming a global hub for green hydrogen. (10 marks, 150 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment; Infrastructure: Energy; Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
mainsMocks.approach: Briefly introduce India's green hydrogen mission. Discuss opportunities like abundant renewable energy potential, strategic location, and demand from hard-to-abate sectors. Address challenges such as high production costs, technological gaps (e.g., electrolysers), infrastructure requirements (storage, transport), and water availability. Conclude with policy recommendations and the long-term vision.

quickBytes.text: The 'National Urban Renewal Mission 2.0' targets 100 cities for transformation into 'Green & Smart Hubs' over five years.
India is hosting the second G20 Sherpa meeting in Udaipur, championing the Global South agenda.
RBI's Monetary Policy Committee maintained the repo rate at 6.5% for the current review.
India aims for 15 million tonnes of green hydrogen production capacity by 2030 under its national mission.
ISRO's 'Shukrayaan-1' successfully performed its Trans-Venus Injection maneuver, heading towards Venus.
The Supreme Court upheld the constitutional validity of 'One Nation, One Election' recommendations.
INS Visakhapatnam-II, the first Project 15B stealth destroyer, was inducted into the Indian Navy.
NEP 2020 implementation is in an advanced phase, focusing on integrating skill development from Class 6 onwards.
UPI transactions crossed 15 billion in January 2025, marking a new milestone for digital payments.
ASI unearthed ancient Buddhist monastery remains in Jajpur, Odisha, believed to be part of Pushpagiri Mahavihara.

GS1.headline: ASI Unearths Ancient Buddhist Monastery Remains in Odisha, Boosting Heritage Tourism
GS1.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) announced the discovery of extensive remains of an ancient Buddhist monastery in Odisha's Jajpur district.
• The excavations revealed stupas, prayer halls, residential cells, and numerous artifacts dating back to the 7th-10th centuries CE.
• This discovery sheds new light on the spread of Buddhism in eastern India and the region's rich cultural heritage.
• The site is believed to be part of the ancient 'Pushpagiri Mahavihara' complex, a significant learning center mentioned in historical texts.
• The state government plans to develop the site as a major heritage tourism destination, attracting scholars and tourists alike.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.

Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including water-bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes.
prelimsPoints: ASI unearthed ancient Buddhist monastery in Jajpur, Odisha.
Remains include stupas, prayer halls, artifacts from 7th-10th centuries CE.
Believed to be part of Pushpagiri Mahavihara.
Discovery sheds light on Buddhism in eastern India.
Site to be developed for heritage tourism.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of recent archaeological discoveries in understanding India's ancient history and cultural linkages.
Analyze the role of Buddhist monasteries as centers of learning and their contribution to Indian art and architecture.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in preserving and promoting archaeological sites for heritage tourism.
Examine the potential socio-economic impact of developing such sites on local communities.
GS1.imageDescription: Archaeologists carefully excavating ancient ruins, revealing stone structures and artifacts at the site.
GS1.trivia: Odisha, historically known as Kalinga, was a significant center for the spread of Buddhism in ancient times, with many monastic complexes.

GS2.headline: PM Launches 'National Urban Renewal Mission 2.0' for Sustainable City Development
GS2.explainer:
• Prime Minister unveils an ambitious second phase of the urban development mission, focusing on climate-resilient infrastructure and smart governance.
• The mission aims to transform 100 cities into 'Green & Smart Hubs' over the next five years, integrating renewable energy and digital solutions.
• Key pillars include integrated waste management, circular economy principles, green mobility solutions, and enhanced public-private partnerships.
• Funding mechanisms include a mix of central assistance, state contributions, and innovative municipal bonds tied to green projects.
• Emphasis on citizen participation and capacity building for urban local bodies to ensure long-term sustainability.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
Urbanization, their problems and their remedies
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: National Urban Renewal Mission 2.0 launched for 100 cities.
Focus on climate-resilient infrastructure, green mobility, waste management.
Funding through central, state, and green municipal bonds.
Aims for 'Green & Smart Hubs' transformation over five years.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolution of India's urban development policies and the need for Mission 2.0.
Analyze the potential impact of green infrastructure and digital solutions on urban livability and sustainability.
Critically evaluate the role of public-private partnerships and citizen participation in achieving mission objectives.
Examine the challenges in implementing large-scale urban transformation projects in diverse Indian cities.
GS2.imageDescription: Prime Minister addressing a conference on sustainable urban development, with a digital rendering of a smart city in the background.
GS2.trivia: India is projected to have 600 million people living in urban areas by 2030, making sustainable urban planning critical for its future.

GS2.headline: India Hosts G20 Sherpa Meeting Ahead of Annual Summit, Focus on Global South Agenda
GS2.explainer:
• India chairs the second G20 Sherpa meeting in Udaipur, setting the agenda for the upcoming annual leaders' summit.
• Discussions primarily focused on strengthening multilateral institutions, climate finance for developing nations, and food security.
• India reiterated its commitment to amplifying the voice of the Global South within the G20 framework.
• Key outcomes include consensus building on sustainable development goals and a roadmap for digital public infrastructure sharing.
• The meeting also addressed global economic stability amidst ongoing geopolitical challenges and supply chain disruptions.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
prelimsPoints: Second G20 Sherpa meeting held in Udaipur, chaired by India.
Agenda includes climate finance, food security, multilateral institution reform.
India championing the voice of the Global South.
Consensus on SDGs and Digital Public Infrastructure sharing roadmap.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's increasing role in global governance through platforms like G20.
Discuss the significance of India's 'Global South' agenda in shaping international discourse and policy.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities for G20 in addressing contemporary global economic and environmental issues.
Examine how digital public infrastructure can be leveraged for global sustainable development.
GS2.imageDescription: Delegates from G20 nations participating in a high-level meeting in a conference hall.
GS2.trivia: The G20 represents around two-thirds of the world's population, 85% of global GDP, and over 75% of global trade.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Validity of 'One Nation, One Election' Committee Recommendations
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a landmark ruling, has affirmed the constitutional validity of the 'One Nation, One Election' committee's recommendations.
• The five-judge constitutional bench stated that simultaneous elections align with the spirit of cooperative federalism, provided certain constitutional amendments are enacted.
• The judgment emphasizes the need for broad political consensus and extensive public deliberation before full implementation.
• Arguments for the move include significant cost savings, reduced burden on administrative machinery, and continuity of policy.
• Critics' concerns regarding potential impact on federal structure and regional parties were acknowledged, with the court suggesting safeguards.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act.
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court upheld constitutional validity of 'One Nation, One Election' recommendations.
Requires constitutional amendments and political consensus.
Arguments: cost savings, administrative efficiency, policy continuity.
Court acknowledged concerns regarding federal structure and regional parties.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the pros and cons of implementing 'One Nation, One Election' in India.
Discuss the constitutional challenges and amendments required for simultaneous elections.
Evaluate the potential impact of simultaneous elections on India's federal structure and the dynamics of regional politics.
Examine the role of the Election Commission of India and other stakeholders in facilitating such a reform.
GS2.imageDescription: A gavel resting on law books in front of the Supreme Court of India emblem.
GS2.trivia: India held simultaneous elections for Lok Sabha and State Assemblies until 1967.

GS2.headline: New National Education Policy Implementation Enters Advanced Phase with Focus on Skill Development
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education announces the advanced phase of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 implementation, prioritizing skill development.
• New guidelines issued for integrating vocational education and skill training from the school level (Class 6 onwards) into higher education.
• Emphasis on practical learning, internships, and industry partnerships to make graduates job-ready and foster entrepreneurship.
• The National Credit Framework (NCrF) is being fully operationalized to facilitate seamless mobility between academic and vocational streams.
• Teacher training programs are being revamped to equip educators with skills for multidisciplinary and experiential learning approaches.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 implementation enters advanced phase, focusing on skill development.
Vocational education from Class 6, integration into higher education.
National Credit Framework (NCrF) fully operationalized.
Emphasis on internships and industry partnerships.
mainsPoints: Discuss the core objectives of NEP 2020 regarding skill development and its potential to address India's demographic dividend.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in integrating vocational education into the mainstream curriculum.
Evaluate the significance of the National Credit Framework (NCrF) in promoting flexibility and multidisciplinary learning.
Examine the role of teacher training and industry collaboration in successful implementation of skill-based education.
GS2.imageDescription: Students engaged in hands-on vocational training in a modern classroom setting.
GS2.trivia: The NEP 2020 replaced the 1986 National Policy on Education, aiming for a holistic, flexible, and multidisciplinary education system.

GS2.headline: India-EU Free Trade Agreement Talks Progress Towards Critical Breakthrough
GS2.explainer:
• Negotiations for the India-European Union Free Trade Agreement (FTA) are reportedly nearing a breakthrough on key contentious issues.
• Both sides have made significant progress on market access for goods, services, and investment protection.
• Areas of consensus include intellectual property rights, geographical indications, and sustainable development provisions.
• The FTA is expected to significantly boost bilateral trade and investment, creating new opportunities for businesses.
• Despite initial hurdles, political will from both India and the EU is driving the accelerated pace of negotiations.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India-EU FTA talks nearing breakthrough on key issues.
Progress on market access for goods, services, investment.
Consensus on IPR, GIs, sustainable development provisions.
Aims to boost bilateral trade and investment.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic and economic significance of an India-EU FTA for India's global trade ambitions.
Discuss the potential benefits and challenges for Indian industries and consumers from the FTA.
Evaluate the role of intellectual property rights and sustainable development clauses in modern trade agreements.
Examine how India balances its 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' vision with the pursuit of global trade integration.
GS2.imageDescription: The flags of India and the European Union displayed together, symbolizing cooperation.
GS2.trivia: The EU is one of India's largest trading partners, and an FTA would significantly liberalize trade in goods and services.

GS2.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends Reforms for Lokpal and Lokayukta Act
GS2.explainer:
• A Joint Parliamentary Committee submitted its report recommending significant amendments to the Lokpal and Lokayukta Act, 2013.
• Key recommendations include strengthening the investigative powers of the Lokpal and ensuring its financial and administrative autonomy.
• The panel suggested a more streamlined process for the appointment of Lokpal members and clearer guidelines for their jurisdiction.
• Focus on enhancing transparency and accountability mechanisms for public functionaries, including provisions for quicker disposal of complaints.
• The recommendations aim to make the anti-corruption ombudsman more effective and independent in combating corruption.
gsSubTopics: Statutory, regulatory and various quasi-judicial bodies.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Transparency & Accountability and institutional and other measures.
prelimsPoints: Parliamentary Committee recommended amendments to Lokpal and Lokayukta Act, 2013.
Suggestions to strengthen Lokpal's investigative powers and autonomy.
Streamlined appointment process and clearer jurisdiction.
Aim to enhance transparency and quicker complaint disposal.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolution and challenges in the functioning of the Lokpal and Lokayukta institutions in India.
Analyze the significance of the parliamentary committee's recommendations in strengthening anti-corruption mechanisms.
Evaluate the balance between ensuring autonomy of anti-corruption bodies and maintaining accountability.
Examine how an effective Lokpal can contribute to good governance and public trust.
GS2.imageDescription: The Parliament House of India, symbolizing legislative action and governance.
GS2.trivia: The concept of an ombudsman originated in Sweden in the early 19th century to oversee governmental actions.

GS2.headline: India Deepens Strategic Partnership with African Nations at Bilateral Summit
GS2.explainer:
• India hosted the 5th India-Africa Forum Summit (IAFS) in New Delhi, reaffirming its commitment to a long-term strategic partnership.
• Key agreements were signed across sectors including defense, renewable energy, digital cooperation, and capacity building.
• Prime Minister emphasized India's 'SAGAR' vision and 'Look Africa' policy, promoting shared prosperity and security in the Indian Ocean region.
• Discussions focused on multilateral cooperation, climate change, counter-terrorism, and strengthening South-South cooperation.
• The summit concluded with a joint declaration outlining a roadmap for enhanced engagement and mutual development goals.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: 5th India-Africa Forum Summit (IAFS) held in New Delhi.
Agreements signed in defense, renewable energy, digital cooperation.
India's 'SAGAR' vision and 'Look Africa' policy highlighted.
Focus on climate change, counter-terrorism, South-South cooperation.
Joint declaration for enhanced engagement and development goals.
mainsPoints: Analyze the historical evolution and current significance of India's relations with African nations.
Discuss the pillars of India's 'Look Africa' policy and its alignment with India's geopolitical and economic interests.
Evaluate the impact of India's development cooperation model in Africa, contrasting it with other global powers.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for strengthening India-Africa collaboration in a rapidly changing global order.
GS2.imageDescription: Leaders from India and various African nations shaking hands at the India-Africa Forum Summit.
GS2.trivia: India has extended over $12 billion in concessional credit to African countries, supporting various development projects.

GS2.headline: India and Sri Lanka Enhance Maritime Security Cooperation in Indian Ocean Region
GS2.explainer:
• India and Sri Lanka concluded a high-level dialogue on maritime security, reaffirming their commitment to a free and open Indo-Pacific.
• Discussions focused on intelligence sharing, joint patrols, and capacity building for combating illegal fishing, drug trafficking, and maritime terrorism.
• Both nations emphasized the importance of adhering to international law, including UNCLOS, for regional peace and stability.
• India extended further assistance to the Sri Lankan Navy for training and equipment upgrades.
• The enhanced cooperation is crucial for securing vital sea lanes of communication and addressing common security challenges in the Indian Ocean.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
prelimsPoints: India-Sri Lanka held high-level dialogue on maritime security.
Focus on intelligence sharing, joint patrols, capacity building.
Combatting illegal fishing, drug trafficking, maritime terrorism.
India provided assistance to Sri Lankan Navy.
Crucial for securing sea lanes in Indian Ocean.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving maritime security challenges in the Indian Ocean Region and the role of India-Sri Lanka cooperation.
Analyze the significance of bilateral defense and security partnerships for India's 'Neighbourhood First' policy.
Evaluate the challenges posed by illegal fishing and drug trafficking in the IOR and the effectiveness of current counter-measures.
Examine the importance of UNCLOS in governing maritime activities and ensuring regional stability.
GS2.imageDescription: Naval ships from India and Sri Lanka conducting a joint exercise in the Indian Ocean.
GS2.trivia: The Indian Ocean is one of the busiest shipping lanes in the world, carrying a significant portion of global trade.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate, Signals Cautious Optimism Amidst Global Headwinds
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5%.
• This decision reflects a cautious approach, balancing inflation management with supporting economic growth.
• RBI Governor highlighted improving domestic consumption and investment sentiment, while acknowledging persistent global economic uncertainties.
• Inflation projections for FY25 were revised marginally downwards, attributing to easing supply-side pressures.
• The central bank reiterated its commitment to remaining 'withdrawal of accommodation' to ensure inflation aligns with the target.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Monetary Policy.
prelimsPoints: RBI MPC maintains repo rate at 6.5%.
Inflation projections for FY25 revised downwards.
Policy stance remains 'withdrawal of accommodation'.
Focus on balancing inflation control and growth support.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors influencing RBI's decision to maintain the repo rate and its implications for the Indian economy.
Discuss the challenges faced by the MPC in balancing inflation control with economic growth objectives.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current monetary policy tools in addressing domestic and global economic uncertainties.
Examine the relationship between government fiscal policy and RBI's monetary policy in achieving macroeconomic stability.
GS3.imageDescription: The Reserve Bank of India headquarters building in Mumbai.
GS3.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was constituted in 2016 to bring transparency and accountability in formulating monetary policy.

GS3.headline: India Unveils Aggressive Targets for Green Hydrogen Production by 2030
GS3.explainer:
• Ministry of New and Renewable Energy announces revised ambitious targets under the National Green Hydrogen Mission.
• India aims to achieve 15 million tonnes of green hydrogen production capacity by 2030, significantly increasing from previous targets.
• The move is intended to position India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, reducing reliance on fossil fuels.
• New incentives for electrolyser manufacturing and green hydrogen adoption in hard-to-abate sectors like steel and fertilizers were proposed.
• This initiative is crucial for India's net-zero emissions goal by 2070 and enhancing energy security.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
GS3.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
prelimsPoints: New target: 15 million tonnes green hydrogen production by 2030.
Part of National Green Hydrogen Mission.
Aims to make India a global hub for production and export.
Incentives for electrolyser manufacturing and sectoral adoption.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of green hydrogen for India's energy security and climate goals.
Analyze the economic and technological challenges in scaling up green hydrogen production and adoption in India.
Evaluate the potential of India becoming a global leader in green hydrogen, considering domestic demand and export potential.
Examine the role of government incentives and policy frameworks in driving the green hydrogen ecosystem.
GS3.imageDescription: A stylized graphic showing hydrogen atoms and green energy sources like wind turbines and solar panels.
GS3.trivia: Green hydrogen is produced by splitting water into hydrogen and oxygen using renewable electricity, making it a zero-emission fuel.

GS3.headline: ISRO's 'Shukrayaan-1' Completes Critical Orbital Maneuver Towards Venus
GS3.explainer:
• India's maiden mission to Venus, 'Shukrayaan-1', successfully executed a crucial Trans-Venus Injection (TVI) maneuver.
• This maneuver propels the spacecraft out of Earth's sphere of influence and towards its intended trajectory to Venus.
• ISRO scientists expressed confidence in the mission's progress, which aims to study Venus's atmosphere and surface composition.
• 'Shukrayaan-1' carries 16 scientific payloads from India, France, Sweden, and Germany, making it a truly international collaboration.
• The mission is expected to reach Venus orbit by late 2025, providing valuable data on the planet's geological and atmospheric evolution.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space.
prelimsPoints: ISRO's 'Shukrayaan-1' is India's first Venus mission.
Successfully completed Trans-Venus Injection (TVI) maneuver.
Carries 16 scientific payloads from multiple countries.
Aims to study Venus's atmosphere and surface.
Expected to reach Venus orbit by late 2025.
mainsPoints: Discuss the scientific objectives of ISRO's Shukrayaan-1 mission and its significance for planetary science.
Analyze how international collaborations enhance India's space exploration capabilities and global standing.
Evaluate the technological challenges involved in interplanetary missions and ISRO's advancements in this domain.
Examine the broader implications of such missions for understanding Earth's climate and geological history.
GS3.imageDescription: An artistic rendering of the Shukrayaan-1 spacecraft orbiting Venus, with its scientific instruments deployed.
GS3.trivia: Venus is often called Earth's 'sister planet' due to its similar size and mass, but it has an extremely hot and dense atmosphere.

GS3.headline: Indian Navy Inducts First Indigenous Stealth Destroyer from Project 15B
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Navy commissioned 'INS Visakhapatnam-II', the first of four stealth destroyers built under Project 15B, at Mazagon Dock Shipbuilders Limited.
• This induction marks a significant milestone in India's 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' (Self-Reliant India) initiative in defense manufacturing.
• The destroyer is equipped with advanced stealth features, state-of-the-art weapon systems, and sensors, enhancing India's maritime capabilities.
• Project 15B follows the successful Project 15A (Kolkata-class) destroyers, building on indigenous design and construction expertise.
• The vessel will significantly bolster the Indian Navy's presence in the Indo-Pacific region, contributing to regional security.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
prelimsPoints: INS Visakhapatnam-II, first Project 15B stealth destroyer, commissioned.
Built by Mazagon Dock Shipbuilders Limited (MDL).
Features advanced stealth technology and weapon systems.
Part of 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' in defense manufacturing.
Enhances Indian Navy's capabilities in the Indo-Pacific.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic significance of indigenous defense production for India's national security and geopolitical ambitions.
Analyze the role of Project 15B destroyers in enhancing the Indian Navy's blue-water capabilities and regional deterrence.
Evaluate the progress and challenges in achieving self-reliance in India's defense sector.
Examine the implications of a stronger Indian Navy for stability in the Indo-Pacific region.
GS3.imageDescription: The newly commissioned INS Visakhapatnam-II destroyer sailing in open waters.
GS3.trivia: Stealth technology in naval vessels aims to reduce their radar, infrared, acoustic, and magnetic signatures, making them harder to detect.

GS3.headline: Project Cheetah Expansion: New Batch Arrives at Kuno National Park
GS3.explainer:
• A fresh batch of 12 cheetahs, sourced from South Africa and Namibia, arrived at Kuno National Park, Madhya Pradesh.
• This marks the third phase of Project Cheetah, aiming to establish a viable and self-sustaining cheetah population in India.
• The new arrivals bring the total cheetah count at Kuno to over 30, following earlier successful translocations.
• Intensive monitoring and veterinary care protocols are in place to ensure the acclimatization and survival of the new cheetahs.
• The project is a flagship initiative for biodiversity conservation and restoring ecological balance in Indian grasslands.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Biodiversity and its conservation.
GS3.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: New batch of 12 cheetahs arrived at Kuno National Park.
Part of the third phase of Project Cheetah.
Total cheetah count at Kuno now over 30.
Cheetahs sourced from South Africa and Namibia.
Aims for a self-sustaining cheetah population in India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance and conservation objectives of Project Cheetah in India.
Analyze the challenges involved in reintroducing a species like the cheetah, including habitat management and prey base.
Evaluate the role of international collaboration in wildlife conservation efforts, citing Project Cheetah as an example.
Examine the socio-economic impacts and community involvement aspects of large-scale wildlife projects.
GS3.imageDescription: A newly arrived cheetah cautiously stepping out of its crate into an enclosure at Kuno National Park.
GS3.trivia: Cheetahs were declared extinct in India in 1952, making Project Cheetah a historic effort to bring them back.

GS3.headline: Digital Public Infrastructure: UPI Transactions Cross New Milestones, Boosting Financial Inclusion
GS3.explainer:
• Unified Payments Interface (UPI) recorded over 15 billion transactions in January 2025, reaching a new monthly high.
• This surge highlights the growing adoption of digital payments across urban and rural India, driving financial inclusion.
• The success of UPI is attributed to its user-friendliness, interoperability, and low-cost transaction model.
• Government initiatives like 'Digital India' and 'Jan Dhan Yojana' have created a conducive environment for DPI adoption.
• The expansion of UPI-linked services, including credit on UPI and cross-border payments, continues to enhance its utility.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it.

mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative impact of Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) like UPI on India's financial landscape and economy.
